items.text: Hi Leo, I am at the sports centre now. Tennis starts at 5.30, not 5.00. Please bring your water bottle. — Dan
items.prompt: What should Leo do?
opts: Go to the sports centre later than planned.
Buy a new tennis ball.
Meet Dan at home before tennis.
items.rationale: Dan says tennis starts at 5.30, not 5.00, so Leo should arrive later than originally planned. The message says nothing about buying a ball, and there is no mention of meeting at home.

items.text: Please put books in the blue box after you use them. Do not put them back on the shelves.
items.prompt: Students must
opts: leave used books in a special place.
take books home after school.
ask the teacher for every book.
items.rationale: The notice says to put books in 'the blue box' — a specific place — after using them. There is no mention of taking books home or asking a teacher.

items.text: From Sara: Can you feed my cat on Saturday morning? I am going to visit my aunt. The food is in the kitchen cupboard.
items.prompt: Sara wants someone to
opts: take her cat to her aunt's house.
give food to her cat.
buy cat food on Saturday.
items.rationale: Sara asks someone to 'feed' her cat — give it food. She is visiting her aunt alone (not taking the cat), and the food is already in the cupboard so no shopping is needed.

items.text: Bus 27 does not stop here this week. Please use the stop outside the cinema.
items.prompt: This sign says that Bus 27
opts: is faster this week.
leaves from a different place.
stops outside the cinema only at night.
items.rationale: The sign redirects passengers to a different stop (outside the cinema). Nothing is said about speed or a night-only restriction.

items.text: Mum, I made pasta for dinner. It is in the fridge. Please heat it for three minutes. Tom
items.prompt: What does Tom tell his mum?
opts: The dinner is ready to eat cold.
She needs to cook pasta for three minutes.
She should warm the food before eating it.
items.rationale: Tom says to 'heat it for three minutes' — warm it up. The pasta is already cooked (not cold), and it needs to be reheated, not cooked from scratch.

items.text: Today only: all notebooks are two for the price of one. Pens and bags are not included.
items.prompt: What can customers buy more cheaply today?
opts: notebooks
pens
bags
items.rationale: The offer is specifically on notebooks ('two for the price of one'). The notice explicitly says pens and bags are not included in the offer.

items.text: GRAND MARKET — every Saturday morning at the town square. Fresh fruit, vegetables, and bread. Free parking behind the market.
items.prompt: What does the advertisement say about the market?
opts: It sells food every Saturday.
It is open every day of the week.
Shoppers pay to park their cars.
items.rationale: The market is on every Saturday morning and sells fresh food. It is not open every day, and parking is free, not paid.

items.text: Dear Grandma, We arrived in Paris yesterday. The hotel is beautiful and the food is wonderful. We are visiting the Eiffel Tower tomorrow. Love, Clara
items.prompt: What is Clara doing tomorrow?
opts: arriving in Paris
going to a famous place
eating at a nice restaurant
items.rationale: Clara says she is visiting the Eiffel Tower tomorrow — a famous tourist place. She already arrived in Paris yesterday, and there is no mention of a restaurant visit tomorrow.

items.text: Hi Priya, the swimming pool is closed today because of cleaning. It will open again on Thursday. Sorry! — Jake
items.prompt: What does Jake tell Priya?
opts: She can swim today but must be careful.
The pool will not be open until Thursday.
She should go to a different pool.
items.rationale: Jake says the pool is closed today and will reopen on Thursday. He does not suggest swimming today or going to a different pool.

items.text: School trip to the Science Museum — Tuesday 14 March. Students must bring a packed lunch. No money is needed.
items.prompt: What must students bring on the school trip?
opts: money for food
their own lunch
a science book
items.rationale: The notice says students must bring a packed lunch. It clearly states that no money is needed, and there is no mention of bringing a science book.

items.text: Gym changing rooms are closed for repair until Friday. Please use the changing rooms near the swimming pool.
items.prompt: What does the sign tell gym users?
opts: The gym is closed until Friday.
They should change in a different room.
The swimming pool is also being repaired.
items.rationale: The sign says the gym changing rooms are closed, so users should use the ones near the pool instead. The gym itself is not closed — only the changing rooms. There is no mention of the pool being repaired.

items.text: From: Aunt Rosa. Hi, I am coming to visit on Sunday. Can you ask your mum to make her apple cake? It is my favourite!
items.prompt: What does Aunt Rosa want?
opts: to bring a cake on Sunday
someone to bake a special cake for her
to learn how to make apple cake
items.rationale: Aunt Rosa asks the recipient to get their mum to make apple cake for her visit. She is not bringing a cake herself, and she is not asking for a recipe.

items.text: City Zoo opening hours: Monday–Friday 9 am to 5 pm. Weekends 9 am to 7 pm. Last entry is one hour before closing.
items.prompt: When is the last time visitors can enter the zoo on a Saturday?
opts: at 7 pm
at 6 pm
at 5 pm
items.rationale: On weekends the zoo closes at 7 pm, but the last entry is one hour before closing, so the last entry on Saturday is 6 pm. Visitors cannot enter at 7 pm (that is closing time) or 5 pm (the weekday closing time).

items.text: Dad, your friend Marco called. He wants you to call him back before 8 o'clock tonight. His number is 07765 432 100.
items.prompt: What should Dad do?
opts: Wait for Marco to call again.
Phone Marco before 8 pm.
Send Marco a text message.
items.rationale: The note says Marco wants Dad to call him back before 8 o'clock. There is no suggestion of waiting for another call or sending a text.

items.text: HEALTHY BITES CAFÉ — homemade soups, salads, and smoothies. Open Monday to Saturday, 8 am to 4 pm. No hot meals served after 3 pm.
items.prompt: What do we know about Healthy Bites Café?
opts: It serves hot food all day.
Hot meals are only available until 3 pm.
It is open seven days a week.
items.rationale: The advertisement states that no hot meals are served after 3 pm. Hot food is not available all day, and the café is open Monday to Saturday — six days, not seven.

items.text: Hey Sam, can you bring the football to school tomorrow? I forgot mine at home and we have PE in the afternoon. — Chris
items.prompt: Why does Chris need Sam to bring the football?
opts: Chris left his football at school.
They have a sports class tomorrow.
Sam asked Chris to play football.
items.rationale: Chris needs the football because they have PE in the afternoon. He forgot his own football at home (not at school), and there is no mention of Sam inviting Chris to play.

items.text: Hi Mum, the weather here in Rome is hot and sunny every day. I am eating so much pizza and gelato! I fly home on Friday. Miss you! — Bella
items.prompt: What is the weather like in Rome?
opts: warm and dry
hot and rainy
cool and sunny
items.rationale: Bella says the weather is 'hot and sunny' — warm and dry. There is no mention of rain, and 'cool' would contradict 'hot'.

items.text: Lost: a small brown dog called Biscuit. Last seen near the park on Green Street on Monday evening. Please call 07800 111 222 if you find him.
items.prompt: What does the notice ask people to do?
opts: Look for the dog in the park every day.
Telephone if they see the dog.
Bring the dog to Green Street.
items.rationale: The notice asks people to call a number if they find the dog. It does not ask them to visit the park daily or bring the dog to Green Street.

items.text: TRAIN PLATFORM 3 — Long-distance trains only. Local trains depart from Platform 1 and Platform 2.
items.prompt: Which trains leave from Platform 3?
opts: trains to nearby towns
trains to far-away places
all trains at this station
items.rationale: Platform 3 is for long-distance trains — trains that go to far-away places. Local (nearby) trains use Platforms 1 and 2, and not all trains use Platform 3.

items.text: From: Dr Lee's clinic. Your appointment is on Wednesday 22 June at 10:30 am. Please arrive 10 minutes early to complete a short form.
items.prompt: What should the patient do before the appointment?
opts: Come a little earlier than 10:30 am.
Call the clinic to confirm the date.
Bring a form from home.
items.rationale: The email asks the patient to arrive 10 minutes early to fill in a form at the clinic. There is no request to call to confirm, and the form is at the clinic, not brought from home.

items.text: Library Wi-Fi password: ReadMore2024. The password changes on the first day of every month. Laptops and tablets welcome.
items.prompt: What does the card tell us about the Wi-Fi password?
opts: It is the same all year.
It changes every month.
You need to ask a librarian for it.
items.rationale: The card says the password changes on the first day of every month. The password is printed on the card so there is no need to ask the librarian, and it clearly does not stay the same all year.

items.text: Please water the plants in the living room every two days. The ones in the bedroom only need water once a week. — Mia
items.prompt: How often should the living-room plants be watered?
opts: every day
every two days
once a week
items.rationale: Mia says to water the living-room plants every two days. Daily watering is too frequent, and once a week is what the bedroom plants need, not the living-room ones.

items.text: BIKE HIRE — £5 per hour or £15 for a full day. Helmets are provided free of charge. Book online or at our shop on Market Street.
items.prompt: What is included in the price of hiring a bike?
opts: a map of cycling routes
a free helmet
a lock for the bike
items.rationale: The advertisement says helmets are provided free of charge. There is no mention of maps or locks being included in the price.

items.text: Hi Gran, we are coming on Saturday not Sunday. Dad is working on Sunday now. See you at 2 pm! — Lucy
items.prompt: Why is the family visiting on Saturday instead of Sunday?
opts: Gran asked them to come earlier.
Lucy's dad has to work on Sunday.
The journey is shorter on Saturday.
items.rationale: Lucy says her dad is working on Sunday, so they changed the visit to Saturday. There is no mention of Gran requesting a change or the journey length.

items.text: SUPERMARKET SELF-CHECKOUT — maximum 10 items. For more than 10 items, please use the checkouts on the left.
items.prompt: Who can use the self-checkout?
opts: shoppers with ten items or fewer
all shoppers in the supermarket
shoppers who pay by card only
items.rationale: The sign says the self-checkout is for a maximum of 10 items, so only shoppers with ten items or fewer can use it. All shoppers are not eligible, and payment method is not mentioned.

items.text: Dear Joe, I am at a ski resort in Austria. I tried skiing for the first time yesterday — it was difficult but fun! I will practise again tomorrow. — Anna
items.prompt: What does Anna say about skiing?
opts: She has skied here before.
She found it hard but enjoyed it.
She will not ski again tomorrow.
items.rationale: Anna says it was 'difficult but fun' — hard but enjoyable. It was her first time, so she has not skied here before, and she plans to practise again tomorrow.

items.text: Students: the school café will be closed next Monday for a staff training day. A cold lunch box will be provided. Please order yours by Friday.
items.prompt: What must students do before Friday?
opts: bring their own lunch on Monday
order a lunch box
go to a different café
items.rationale: The notice says students must order their lunch box by Friday. They will receive a provided cold lunch box — they do not need to bring their own or find another café.

items.text: Greenfield Community Pool — Adult swim: 6 am to 8 am daily. Children must be accompanied by an adult at all times.
items.prompt: What rule must children follow at the pool?
opts: They can only swim in the morning.
They must always be with a grown-up.
They must book a place in advance.
items.rationale: The card states that children must be accompanied by an adult at all times. The 6–8 am session is for adults only, and there is no mention of booking in advance.

items.text: From: Tom. Hi, are you free on Friday evening? There is a new Thai restaurant near my office. I heard it is very good. Let me know!
items.prompt: What is Tom suggesting?
opts: going to a restaurant together on Friday
cooking Thai food at his office
trying a restaurant he has already visited
items.rationale: Tom is suggesting they go to the new Thai restaurant on Friday evening. The restaurant is near his office but not at it, and he says he 'heard' it is good — meaning he has not been there yet.

items.text: Hi, this is the dentist's office. Your appointment on Thursday at 3 pm has been cancelled. We will call you to make a new one. Sorry for the inconvenience.
items.prompt: What does the message say about the appointment?
opts: It has been moved to a new time on Thursday.
It will not happen and a new one will be arranged.
The patient needs to call the dentist to cancel.
items.rationale: The message says the appointment is cancelled and the dentist's office will call to make a new one. It has not simply been moved to another Thursday time, and the patient does not need to call — the office will call them.

items.text: MOUNTAIN VIEW HOTEL — rooms from £60 per night. Breakfast included. Free shuttle bus to the ski slopes every morning at 8 am.
items.prompt: What is included in the hotel price?
opts: dinner each evening
morning meal and bus to the slopes
a ski pass for each guest
items.rationale: The price includes breakfast (morning meal) and a free shuttle bus to the ski slopes. Dinner and ski passes are not mentioned as included.
